channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Reports Rising Inflation, Water Budget, and Mixed Stock Exchange Performance in April 2026
item.description: During April 2026, Tanzania experienced a rise in inflation to 4%, mainly driven by increasing fuel and transport costs, putting pressure on household expenses. In investment news, Dangote opted for K...

item.title: Asian stocks dip amid rising US inflation and uncertain Iran ceasefire
item.description: Asian stock markets opened lower on May 13, 2026, as investors reacted to a hotter-than-expected US inflation report and ongoing tensions in the Middle East. MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shar...

item.title: KU Teachers Strike Over Pending Financial Benefits
item.description: KARACHI: Teachers at University of Karachi (KU) have called off their routine teaching and examinations for the past week, demanding long-overdue financial benefits. The strike, led by KU Teachers' As...

item.title: Behind the Scenes with David Bowie on the Set of 'The Man Who Fell to Earth', 1975
item.description: In 1975, iconic photographer Terry O’Neill captured David Bowie in Los Angeles during the filming of Nicolas Roeg’s sci-fi classic, 'The Man Who Fell to Earth.' The photographs reveal Bowie in a detac...

item.title: Canelands Man Injured in Grocery Robbery
item.description: A 26-year-old man from Canelands, KwaZulu-Natal, was stabbed and robbed on Saturday evening while returning from Verulam's central business district with three friends. The group had just stepped off ...

item.title: Izo Motors Introduces Modern Batteries and Provides Employment to 50+ Youths
item.description: Izo Motors, a local automotive company in Tanzania, recently unveiled its new line of modern batteries designed for electric vehicles. This launch is not only a step forward in adopting cleaner and mo...

item.title: Ibrahim Mafia loses WBC Africa bantamweight title due to rule breaches
item.description: Tanzanian boxer Ibrahim Mafia has officially lost his WBC Africa bantamweight title after his defeat to Filipino fighter Alvin Camique in a non-title bout in Dar es Salaam. The World Boxing Council (W...

item.title: Tinubu urges investors to explore Nigeria’s maritime economy at Africa-France summit
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don waka go Kenya to hail global investors make dem tap into Nigeria’s blue economy as government dey push reforms. For the Africa/France summit wey dem co-host with President Em...

item.title: Baroka chairman urges players to return to training despite salary delays
item.description: Baroka FC chairman Khurishi Mphahlele has issued a stern warning to his players following a strike over delayed salaries. The club, recently relegated from the Motsepe Foundation Championship to the A...

item.title: Gbenga Daniel Confident of Winning Ogun East Senatorial Primary Against Abiodun
item.description: Former Ogun State Governor and current senator for Ogun East, Otunba Gbenga Daniel, don talk say e go beat Governor Dapo Abiodun well well for the coming APC senatorial primary wey dey set before 2027...

item.title: Keir Starmer faces mounting internal pressure but remains UK Prime Minister
item.description: Following disappointing local election results in England, Scotland, and Wales, Prime Minister Keir Starmer has faced intense scrutiny from within his own party. A speech intended to reset his leaders...

item.title: Pima County Board Refers Perjury Allegations Against Sheriff Chris Nanos to AG After Failed Removal Attempt
item.description: A motion to remove Pima County Sheriff Chris Nanos from office failed during a recent Board of Supervisors meeting, though the board voted unanimously to refer allegations of perjury to the state atto...

item.title: Daily Horoscope Guidance for May 13, 2026
item.description: Today’s horoscopes provide guidance and insights for each zodiac sign, helping readers navigate challenges and opportunities on May 13, 2026. For those celebrating their birthday, Mercury and Jupiter ...

item.title: Shepherd Bushiri comments on media closures, SAPS arrests, and immigration challenges
item.description: Self-proclaimed prophet Shepherd Bushiri has shared his perspectives on several pressing issues in South Africa, including the decline of traditional print media, corruption within the South African P...

item.title: Senator Lacson expresses disappointment over Legarda joining Cayetano in Senate leadership change
item.description: Senator Panfilo “Ping” Lacson expressed disappointment following the unexpected leadership shift in the Philippine Senate, where Senator Loren Legarda joined the faction supporting Senator Alan Peter ...

item.title: Civilian awarded Sitara-e-Shujaat for preventing suicide bombing near Kohat
item.description: In a solemn recognition of bravery, President Asif Ali Zardari has approved the award of Sitara-e-Shujaat for Liaqat Shaheed, a retired railway employee, who sacrificed his life while averting a suici...

item.title: NBI urges Senator Bato dela Rosa to surrender amid ICC arrest warrant
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) appealed to Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa to surrender peacefully following an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court (ICC). Despite cal...

item.title: Benoni man arrested for producing and selling fraudulent documents
item.description: A 38-year-old undocumented foreign national was arrested in Benoni on May 12, 2026, after police discovered he had been producing and selling fraudulent documents. The initial tip-off came from the Ek...

item.title: A Look at Leila Hyams’ Career and Vintage Postcards from the 1920s–1930s
item.description: Leila Hyams (1905–1977) was a celebrated American actress and model who gained fame during the 1920s and 1930s, bridging the era from silent films to early talkies. Born into a show-business family in...

item.title: Ramaphosa Highlights Tourism as a Tool for Rural Development and Job Creation
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa addressed delegates at the Africa Travel Indaba in Durban, emphasising tourism as a key driver for economic growth across Africa. He highlighted that tourism should not be se...
